comment.body:
TAIL EVENT:
when market is heavily skewed bullish and when plp aren't leveraged on VIX anymore, dealers aren't hedged against tail risk. to forseeing a tail event look for expansion of size in deep OTM VIX calls, sudden high convexities, margin puts in SPX... so when the momentum in the tail of the distribution curve expands

comment.body:
one can have two position at once.. march long put, and 0dte long call.
but as written, only a fraction of the profit form that call trade, so you can tell that the conviction is lower.
the speculation behind is that vanna flip is oscillating, not a stationary point as i keep saying. what i mark as zero vanna is a spec. calc. centroid value of the set where the zero line can be found with the highest % probabilty.
her vanna flips from positive to negative to the downside (bcs we are in the center of a MM long fly)
if vol up, the flip point moves upwards, if vol down, the flip moves downwards a bit and flattens the curve, thus the contrast between the two regime. 
but if vol stay high bcs certain participants are triggered, and add to vol, it can cause a battle near to the flip, like a magnet, but the poetry is that here vol shold calm down if starts to goes up, in order to break above the flip.
i see some chance for that, but not as big as betting on it with high money.

For those who still trade:

to the upside, zero vanna shifted up to 5958, you need low vol to break that.

comment.body:
Right I get that, and one can have two positions with two different expirations heading in the same direction. Given what you said you then entered a trade immediately after. When you say “next stop is 5927” in the context of very short term and then enter a trade, it isn’t unreasonable to assume you are executing a short term trade in that direction. And now price is at 5924.

At any rate, nice work with the levels, but it is sometimes very confusing trying to decipher what you say into a practical action. I understand you have a lot on your plate but these details are important.
